 {"id":"bd-1eho","title":"Feature: Auto-Tagging + Duplicate Detection (Metadata Assistant)","description":"## Background
As the prompt library grows, manual tagging becomes inconsistent and duplicates sneak in. Pro users need fast, reliable metadata and dedupe support.

## Scope
- Auto-suggest tags, categories, and short descriptions using deterministic rules + semantic similarity (no SKILL.md).
- Duplicate/near-duplicate detection with similarity thresholds and preview diffs.
- Merge flow: mark as alias, merge tags, or ignore with rationale.
- CLI: `jfp tags suggest <prompt>` and `jfp dedupe scan` (Pro-only).

## Deliverables
- Metadata suggestion service + similarity index.
- UI panel for suggestions and duplicate resolution.
- CLI scan output with JSON option.
- Admin override for global tag mappings.

## Test Requirements
### Unit Tests
- Similarity scoring thresholds and tag suggestion rules.
- Duplicate detection determinism.

### Integration Tests
- Suggestions saved to prompt metadata and reflected in search.
- Dedupe merge updates references (packs/workflows/collections).

### E2E Tests
- Create prompt -> receive suggestions -> accept -> verify tags.
- Detect duplicate -> merge -> verify references updated.

## Success Criteria
- Tag consistency improves without manual rework.
- Duplicate prompts are surfaced and resolvable.
- Search quality improves for Pro users.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoFinch","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:48.758255166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:34:31.681132887Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:34:31.681111768Z","close_reason":"Admin tag mapping overrides shipped in 0c67683","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1gf6","title":"Docs: list jfp packs under Premium commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T20:15:08.507514242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:15:19.353380482Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:15:19.353355546Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gf6","depends_on_id":"bd-kfuj","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-02-01T20:15:08.507514242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1ppu","title":"Backend: Budget persistence + hard stop enforcement (Premium)","description":"Implement premium backend storage + enforcement for budget settings. Scope: API endpoints to read/write budget caps + alerts, persist per-user settings, enforce hard-stop on overages for prompt runs, and expose audit log for alerts. Mirror bead in premium repo and link cross-repo dependencies.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:04:43.856815347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:03.903998969Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Draft backend spec for budgets/hard stop (premium repo):

Data model:
- budgets: user_id (pk), monthly_cap_usd, per_run_cap_usd, alerts_enabled, hard_stop_enabled, created_at, updated_at.
- budget_alerts: id, user_id, alert_type (per_run|monthly|hard_stop), cap_usd, total_cost_usd, currency, prompt_id, prompt_title, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, source (estimate|actual), created_at.
- usage_rollups: user_id, month (YYYY-MM), total_cost_usd, total_tokens, last_run_at.

API (premium):
- GET /cli/budgets -> { budgets, usage_month, usage, alerts_enabled, hard_stop_enabled }.
- PUT /cli/budgets -> update caps/toggles; return saved payload.
- GET /cli/budget-alerts?limit=&before= -> list alerts for UI/CLI.
- POST /cli/budget-alerts -> optional client-side logging for cost estimates (optional).

Enforcement:
- On any prompt run/execute endpoint, compute projected cost. If hard_stop_enabled and projected monthly total > cap -> return 402/403 with code budget_exceeded and include caps + current usage.
- If per_run cap exceeded, allow run but record alert (and return warning in response).
- Monthly usage should be computed from actual usage only (estimates should not increment usage).

Client integration notes (public repo):
- CLI currently logs alerts locally to ~/.config/jfp/budget-alerts.jsonl.
- Once backend exists, update CLI status to pull budgets/usage from /cli/budgets for premium users; local config remains fallback.
- Web budget settings page is local-only; wire to backend when endpoints exist.","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:03Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1ppu","title":"Backend: Budget persistence + hard stop enforcement (Premium)","description":"Implement premium backend storage + enforcement for budget settings. Scope: API endpoints to read/write budget caps + alerts, persist per-user settings, enforce hard-stop on overages for prompt runs, and expose audit log for alerts. Mirror bead in premium repo and link cross-repo dependencies.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:04:43.856815347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:17:00.276157174Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Draft backend spec for budgets/hard stop (premium repo):

Data model:
- budgets: user_id (pk), monthly_cap_usd, per_run_cap_usd, alerts_enabled, hard_stop_enabled, created_at, updated_at.
- budget_alerts: id, user_id, alert_type (per_run|monthly|hard_stop), cap_usd, total_cost_usd, currency, prompt_id, prompt_title, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, source (estimate|actual), created_at.
- usage_rollups: user_id, month (YYYY-MM), total_cost_usd, total_tokens, last_run_at.

API (premium):
- GET /cli/budgets -> { budgets, usage_month, usage, alerts_enabled, hard_stop_enabled }.
- PUT /cli/budgets -> update caps/toggles; return saved payload.
- GET /cli/budget-alerts?limit=&before= -> list alerts for UI/CLI.
- POST /cli/budget-alerts -> optional client-side logging for cost estimates (optional).

Enforcement:
- On any prompt run/execute endpoint, compute projected cost. If hard_stop_enabled and projected monthly total > cap -> return 402/403 with code budget_exceeded and include caps + current usage.
- If per_run cap exceeded, allow run but record alert (and return warning in response).
- Monthly usage should be computed from actual usage only (estimates should not increment usage).

Client integration notes (public repo):
- CLI currently logs alerts locally to ~/.config/jfp/budget-alerts.jsonl.
- Once backend exists, update CLI status to pull budgets/usage from /cli/budgets for premium users; local config remains fallback.
- Web budget settings page is local-only; wire to backend when endpoints exist.","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:03Z"},{"id":20,"issue_id":"bd-1ppu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Additional spec details:

Response shapes:
- GET /cli/budgets -> { ok: true, data: { budgets: { monthlyCapUsd, perRunCapUsd, alertsEnabled, hardStopEnabled }, usage: { month, totalCostUsd, totalTokens, lastRunAt } } }
- PUT /cli/budgets (body: partial budgets) -> same payload as GET.
- Error (budget hard stop): status 402 or 403 with { ok:false, error:{ code:"budget_exceeded", message, caps:{ monthlyCapUsd, perRunCapUsd }, usage:{ month, totalCostUsd }, projected:{ totalCostUsd, inputTokens, outputTokens, model } } }.
- Warning only (per-run over cap, hard stop disabled): include { warning:{ code:"budget_warning", message, capUsd, totalCostUsd } } in normal response.

Rollups:
- usage_rollups should be updated on actual billable runs only.
- Use UTC month boundaries; month stored as YYYY-MM string for deterministic queries.

Client expectations:
- CLI/web should treat missing /cli/budgets endpoint as "not available" and keep local-only UI/alerts.
- CLI can show budget status in `jfp status` only when endpoint returns data; otherwise fallback to config-only summary.","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:17:00Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-2auv","title":"Feature: Pro Cost Estimator + Budget Alerts (Web + CLI)","description":"## Background
Pro users need predictable costs and time budgeting. They want to see estimated token usage and set monthly caps before running prompts.

## Scope
- Deterministic token + cost estimator per prompt + model (web + CLI).
- UI badges showing estimated tokens and cost; compare estimate vs actual after runs.
- Budget configuration (monthly cap + per-run threshold) with alerts.
- CLI: `jfp cost <prompt>` and `jfp run --estimate`, plus budget status in `jfp status`.
- Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content).

## Deliverables
- Shared pricing table + cost calculation utility.
- UI components + settings page for budgets and alerts.
- CLI commands and Pro-gated upgrade messaging.
- Alert logging/audit trail for over-budget events.

## Test Requirements
### Unit Tests
- Cost calculation across models and price tiers.
- Budget threshold logic and alert formatting.

### Integration Tests
- Estimator uses prompt variables and produces stable output.
- Budget updates persist and are read by CLI.

### E2E Tests
- Set budget -> estimate -> exceed -> alert shown in UI and CLI.

## Success Criteria
- Estimates within acceptable tolerance vs actual token usage.
- Users can see cost before running prompts.
- Budget alerts prevent surprise overages.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:36.190844227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:09:50.428606684Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:04:28.450652742Z","close_reason":"Implemented in 116012f (budget settings UI + CLI budget alerts); backend persistence/hard-stop tracked separately","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":16,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Web UI cost badges implemented in commit fc824eb. CostBadge added to PromptCard and PromptDetailModal. Remaining: budget settings page and alert logging.","created_at":"2026-01-31T06:33:35Z"},{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Session 2026-01-31: Added CostBadge tests (commit 3e83b39). Web badges complete. Remaining work: budget settings page requires backend/database infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T06:46:06Z"},{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added budget alert log summary to jfp status (JSON + human output). Pending tests (requires explicit delete approval).","created_at":"2026-02-01T21:09:50Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2txr","title":"E2E Tests: History UI Components","description":"## Task
Add UI-level E2E tests for the Recently Viewed History feature.

## Context
The API-level E2E tests exist (e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts) but UI component tests are missing.

## Test Scenarios
1. **RecentlyViewedSidebar Component**
   - Sidebar displays recent items
   - Clear history button works
   - Items link to correct pages
   - Empty state displays correctly
   - Loading state shows correctly

2. **History Page (/history)**
   - Page loads and displays items
   - Search/filter works
   - Clear all history works
   - Timestamps display correctly
   - Links navigate correctly

3. **History Tracking Integration**
   - Viewing a prompt adds to history
   - Search queries are tracked
   - Deduplication prevents rapid duplicates
   - Items appear in sidebar after viewing

## Files to Modify
- e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts (add UI tests)
- e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts (add UI selectors)

## Success Criteria
- All sidebar interactions tested
- History page fully tested
- Integration with tracking verified","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralStar","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:45:40.323914674Z","created_by":"CoralStar","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005730906Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005649042Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-2txr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete:
- Added 16 new UI tests (34 total including API tests)
- History Page UI tests: header, empty state, items display, search filter, clear button, navigation
- RecentlyViewedSidebar tests: title, empty state, history link, items display, 8-item limit
- History Tracking Integration tests: cross-page sync, clear sync, search query display
- Updated history-helpers.ts with better UI selectors for sidebar and history page
- 68 total tests (34 × 2 browser configs: chromium + Mobile Chrome)","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:39Z"}]}
